title: TOLC-MED: Is the biology section easier than IMAT?
content: Hi, I'm Luca. I'm preparing for the TOLC-MED in April. For those who have seen both, do you think the biology part is simpler than the IMAT one? Or is it just me?
author: Luca

answers.author: Emre
answers.content: I'd say it's more straightforward but also more time-pressured. You have less time per question in the TOLC-MED usually.

answers.author: Lina
answers.content: I agree with Emre. The questions are less 'trickier' than IMAT but the clock is always ticking!

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: One big difference is the number of questions. TOLC has more focus on specific topics in human biology.

answers.author: Giulia
answers.content: Exactly! Make sure to study human physiology well for the TOLC-MED.

answers.author: Finn
answers.content: And don't forget the logic part, it's quite different from the IMAT style.

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: Wait, I find the logic part of TOLC easier. Anyone else?

answers.author: Zeynep
answers.content: I wouldn't say easier, just different. More focus on verbal reasoning maybe.

answers.author: Adel
answers.content: Interesting point Zeynep. I'm struggling with the math part of TOLC-MED more than anything else.

title: Anyone from Turkey taking the TOLC-F next month?
content: Hey! I'm Zeynep. I'm taking the pharmacy test in May. Is anyone else here from Turkey planning to go for it? It's a bit stressful with the registration!
author: Zeynep

title: TOLC-B for Biotechnology: Which topics are key?
content: Hello everyone, I'm Hanna. I'm applying for Biotechnology at Pavia. Can anyone tell me the most important biology topics for the TOLC-B?
author: Hanna

answers.author: Alessandro
answers.content: Genetics and molecular biology are definitely the biggest sections. Also, some basic ecology sometimes shows up.

title: TOLC-MED: Can international students take it twice?
content: Hi, I'm Liam. I'm a bit confused about the rules for non-EU students residing abroad. Are we allowed to take the TOLC-MED in both sessions or just once?
author: Liam

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: For non-EU students living abroad, you usually have a specific session or you take the IMAT instead. Better check the official decree!

title: Common traps in TOLC chemistry questions?
content: Hello! I'm Finn. I'm currently reviewing chemistry for the TOLC-MED. What are some of the common traps or difficult topics I should watch out for?
author: Finn

title: CEnT-S portal login issue: 'Session expired'
content: Hi guys, I'm Elif. I'm trying to register for my mock test on the CEnT-S portal, but I keep getting a 'session expired' error every time. Anyone else?
author: Elif

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: Try clearing your browser cache! Or use a different browser, sometimes that helps.

title: How to book a CEnT-S mock test?
content: Hey! I'm Liam. I just signed up for the platform. Can someone tell me how I can book a session for the mock exam? I can't find the link.
author: Liam

answers.author: Lukas
answers.content: Go to the 'Dashboard' and then look for the 'Mock Exams' section. There should be a button to schedule your session there.

title: CEnT-S: Are the mock tests very difficult?
content: Hello, I'm Bence. I'm about to take my first CEnT-S mock test. How difficult are they compared to the actual past papers of the IMAT?
author: Bence

title: IELTS requirement for Italian medical schools?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Madison. What's the minimum IELTS score usually required for med schools in Italy? Is a 6.5 enough for most universities?
author: Madison

answers.author: Noah
answers.content: Yes, 6.5 is the standard requirement. Some might even accept a 6.0, but 6.5 is much safer.

answers.author: Emre
answers.content: Make sure you check the specific university's page, though! Some older decrees were even more relaxed.

title: Universitaly pre-enrolment: When is the deadline?
content: Hello, I'm Olivia. I'm starting my application on Universitaly. When is the deadline for pre-enrolment usually? Is it the same for all med schools?
author: Olivia

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: It's usually around June or July. Better do it as early as possible to avoid any technical glitches!

title: Anyone applying as a transfer student?
content: Hey! I'm Jacob. I'm already in med school here in the US but I'm considering moving to Italy. Is it possible to transfer to an international medical course?
author: Jacob

title: Best way to get a Codice Fiscale?
content: Hi, I'm Emma. I'll be moving to Italy for medical school soon. What's the fastest way to get a Codice Fiscale while I'm still in Canada?
author: Emma

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: You can usually apply for it through the Italian consulate in your city. It's quite straightforward!

title: TOLC-MED: Is the physics section very hard?
content: Hi, I'm Luca. For the TOLC-MED physics part, are the questions very long or more about quick formulas? I'm a bit slow with calculations.
author: Luca

answers.author: Chiara
answers.content: They are usually quite quick if you know the formulas! Practice your mental math to save time.

title: Study resources for TOLC-MED logic?
content: Hello! I'm Finn. I'm looking for some good books or online courses specifically for the logic part of the TOLC-MED. Any recommendations?
author: Finn

title: CEnT-S support response time?
content: Hi guys, I'm Mert. I sent an email to the support team about a payment issue three days ago, but I haven't heard back yet. Is it usually this slow?
author: Mert

answers.author: Lukas
answers.content: It might take up to a week during the busy periods. Maybe try checking your spam folder too?

title: Scholarships for non-EU students in Italy?
content: Hey everyone, I'm Elif. Are there many scholarship opportunities for international students applying for med schools in Italy? Where should I look?
author: Elif

answers.author: Adel
answers.content: Check the EDISU or DSU websites for each region. They offers scholarships based on family income and merit.

title: Applying as a dual citizen (US/Italy)?
content: Hi! I'm Michael. I have both US and Italian citizenship. Should I apply as an EU or as a non-EU student? Which one has more spots usually?
author: Michael

title: TOLC-MED: Can I use a simple calculator?
content: Hello, I'm Hanna. I just wanted to double-check: are we allowed to use a simple calculator during the TOLC-MED? I've heard conflicting info.
author: Hanna

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: No calculators allowed for the TOLC-MED either! You really need to practice your mental arithmetic.

title: Anyone from Canada using the platform?
content: Hi! I'm Emma. Just wondering if there are many other Canadians here using CEnT-S for their med school prep. Would love to hear your experience!
author: Emma

title: Best student city in Italy? (Milan vs Rome vs Bologna)
content: Hey! I'm Liam. I'm trying to decide between Milan, Rome, and Bologna for my med school preference. What's the best city for student life and atmosphere?
author: Liam

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: Bologna and Milan are definitely top choices for student life. Both have a very young and vibrant vibe.

answers.author: Chiara
answers.content: Bologna is much smaller than Milan, so it feels more like a dedicated student city. But Milan has more opportunities maybe.

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: And Rome is just Rome! It's so beautiful and historic, you'll never be bored there.

answers.author: Emre
answers.content: Don't forget about Pavia! It's very close to Milan but much more student-focused and traditional.

answers.author: Lukas
answers.content: This is a tough choice! Every city has its own pros and cons. Think about what you prefer for your daily life.

answers.author: Jacob
answers.content: I'm also leaning towards Bologna because of the student atmosphere mentioned above.

answers.author: Emma
answers.content: Great info guys! I'm also looking into this right now.

answers.author: Finn
answers.content: Another thing to consider is the cost of living. Milan is definitely the most expensive.

answers.author: Lina
answers.content: Good point Finn. Bologna and Pavia are probably a bit more affordable.

title: TOLC-MED biology: Focus on human or plants?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Zeynep. For the TOLC-MED biology part, should I focus more on human biology or are there many questions about plants and general cell theory?
author: Zeynep

answers.author: Emma
answers.content: Focus more on human biology! Cells and genetics are also key, but human physiology is a big part of the test.

title: Dichiarazione di Valore (DOV) process in the US?
content: Hello, I'm Jacob. Can anyone explain the process for getting the DOV from the Italian consulate in the US? How long does it usually take?
author: Jacob

title: TOLC-MED chemistry: Organic chemistry depth?
content: Hi, I'm Luca. How much organic chemistry is usually in the TOLC-MED? Is it just the basic functional groups or more complex reactions too?
author: Luca

answers.author: Alessandro
answers.content: It's mostly the basics—nomenclature, functional groups, and simple reactions. Not as deep as the general chemistry section.

title: CEnT-S discount promo code for the full course?
content: Hey guys, I'm Mert. I saw an ad for a discount on the full prep course. Does anyone have a promo code or know how to apply for it?
author: Mert

title: Anyone applying for Milan IMS (MI-MED) next year?
content: Hi! I'm Sofia. Milan is my top choice for medical school. Is anyone else here applying for the international course there? How are you preparing?
author: Sofia

answers.author: Emma
answers.content: I'm applying there too! I'm mostly using past papers and the CEnT-S mock tests.

title: TOLC-MED: Are the math questions very long?
content: Hello, I'm Bence. I'm quite slow at solving math problems. How many minutes do we usually have per math question in the TOLC-MED test?
author: Bence

answers.author: Lukas
answers.content: You have about 1.5 to 2 minutes per question. It's quite fast, so you need to be very focused and quick!

title: Best areas to find student housing in Milan?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Madison. I'll be moving to Milan next year. What are the best areas for students to find safe and affordable housing? Any specific websites?
author: Madison

title: TOLC-MED logic: Deductive reasoning part?
content: Hey! I'm Liam. I'm currently practicing deductive reasoning for the test. Is it very similar to the ones found in LSAT or GMAT logic sections?
author: Liam

answers.author: David
answers.content: It's a bit simpler than LSAT usually, but the logic is similar. Focus on understanding the relationships between premises and conclusions well.

title: CEnT-S: Anyone from Hungary using the platform?
content: Hi! I'm Zalan. Just wanted to see if there are many other students from Hungary here using the platform for their med school prep. Let's connect!
author: Zalan

title: Visa process for non-EU students in Italy?
content: Hello, I'm Zeynep. What's the best time to start the student visa application for Italy? Should I wait for the final admission results?
author: Zeynep

answers.author: Alessandro
answers.content: Start as soon as you have your pre-enrolment confirmation from Universitaly! The visa process can be quite long, so early is better.

title: TOLC-MED: Are the questions very theoretical?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Mia. For the sciences part of the test, are the questions mostly theoretical or more practical and problem-based?
author: Mia

answers.author: Luca
answers.content: It's a mix of both! You'll need to know the theory well, but also be able to apply it to solve small problems or analyze data.

title: Applying as a dual citizen (US/Italy) for med school?
content: Hey! I'm Michael. I have both US and Italian citizenship. Should I apply as an EU or as a non-EU student? Which one has more spots?
author: Michael

title: TOLC-MED math section difficulty level?
content: Hi, I'm Derya. I'm a bit worried about the math section. How difficult is it compared to other tests like the SAT or GRE?
author: Derya

answers.author: Jacob
answers.content: It's comparable to the SAT math section, maybe a bit more focused on algebra and functions. Practice is definitely key!

title: CEnT-S mobile app for preparation?
content: Hello, I'm Hanna. I have a long commute every day. Is there a mobile app for CEnT-S or is the website mobile-friendly so I can study on the go?
author: Hanna

answers.author: Admin
answers.content: Yes, our platform is fully mobile-responsive! You can access all your courses and mock tests directly from your phone's browser anytime.

title: Applying for a student permit (Permesso di Soggiorno)?
content: Hey! I'm Noah. I'm about to move to Italy for med school. What's the process for getting the Permesso di Soggiorno once I arrive? Is it hard?
author: Noah
